title: Silky Mexican Beef Tacos
description: A show-off Mexican dish featuring tender beef, fresh tomatoes, and a silky texture, all while being Whole30-friendly. This peppery taco recipe is sure to impress your guests.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat the avocado oil over medium-high heat.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Once the beef is browned, add the diced tomatoes, sliced red onion, chopped cilantro, lime juice, ground black pepper, salt, and taco seasoning. Stir well to combine.
Reduce heat to medium-low and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the flavors have melded together and the mixture has reached an internal temperature of 160°F.
While the beef mixture is cooking, prepare the lettuce leaves for serving.
To serve, spoon the beef mixture onto a lettuce leaf and serve immediately.
Garnish with additional chopped cilantro, if desired.
Blanch the lettuce leaves in boiling water for 10-15 seconds to make them more pliable, if desired.
